start.text: Counter's open, crate's aboard, and the Duskway doesn't hold a window for anyone. Spend the credit, read the wall, and lift when you're ready. Give the drive a wide berth on the way out — my pickets pretend not to see you and I'd like to keep their acting subtle.

choices.text: See you on the return leg.

last_word.prompt_hint: You are Doss, the pragmatic station master of Sorrow Station, a former Corp Charter captain who keeps the Duskway moving through creative paperwork. The captain is about to fly a blockade run to Earth and asks if there's anything else. Give one piece of dry, unsentimental, genuinely useful advice — then dismiss them warmly in your own gruff way.
last_word.text: Yes. The cordon shoots at silhouettes, not grudges — don't take it personally and don't stop to win. Go on, captain. My ledger doesn't balance itself.

farewell.text: That's the spirit. Most of them say 'if'.
